exercises.title: GATEKEEPER
promptLines: Define **requirePositive(n)** that returns `n` when `n > 0`, but **throws** an `Error` when `n <= 0`.
starterLines: // TODO: throw when n <= 0, else return n
solutionLines: if (n <= 0) throw new Error('must be positive');
exercises.hint: if (n <= 0) throw new Error(...); return n;
exercises.lore: None shall pass the reactor gate without a positive charge.

exercises.title: CHECKPOINT
exercises.brief: Demand a real handle, trimmed clean.
promptLines: Define **validateName(s)** that returns the name with surrounding whitespace removed.
If the trimmed name is empty (blank or all spaces), **throw** an `Error` instead.
starterLines: // TODO: trim s; throw if empty, else return it
exercises.hint: var name = s.trim(); if (name === '') throw new Error(...); return name;
exercises.lore: A blank ID badge gets you nowhere at the checkpoint.

exercises.title: SAFE PORT
exercises.brief: Divide without ever blowing the fuse.
promptLines: Define **safeDivide(a, b)** that returns `a / b`, but returns `null` instead of dividing by zero.
It must **never throw** — when `b` is `0`, fail safe with `null`.
starterLines: // TODO: return a / b, or null when b is 0
exercises.hint: if (b === 0) return null; return a / b;
exercises.lore: The safe port routes a zero-load to ground instead of frying the deck.

exercises.title: FALLBACK CACHE
exercises.brief: Trust the function, but keep a backup ready.
promptLines: Define **getOrDefault(fn, fallback)** that calls `fn()` and returns its value.
If `fn()` **throws**, swallow the error and return `fallback` instead. Use `try` / `catch`.
starterLines: // TODO: return fn(), or fallback if it throws

tests.name: falls back on throw
tests.code: assertEqual(getOrDefault(function () { throw new Error('boom'); }, 0), 0, 'should fall back to 0');

tests.name: fallback can be a string
tests.code: assertEqual(getOrDefault(function () { throw new Error('down'); }, 'OFFLINE'), 'OFFLINE', 'should fall back to OFFLINE');

tests.name: does not throw on failure
tests.code: assertEqual(getOrDefault(function () { throw new Error('e'); }, null), null, 'should swallow the error');

exercises.hint: try { return fn(); } catch (e) { return fallback; }
exercises.lore: When the live feed dies, the cache keeps the deck breathing.

exercises.title: RANGE LOCK
exercises.brief: Clamp the dial — or trip the alarm.
promptLines: Define **assertRange(x, lo, hi)** that returns `x` when it sits within `lo` and `hi` (inclusive).
starterLines: // TODO: throw if x < lo or x > hi, else return x
solutionLines: if (x < lo || x > hi) throw new Error('out of range');
exercises.hint: if (x < lo || x > hi) throw new Error(...); return x;
exercises.lore: Push the dial past the redline and the range lock slams shut.
